facade_description: 238 Augusta Ave presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion.
